entry.title: Re: cds query for .where(not in)
entry.content:
current cds new version using "not in" 
NOTE: 
IF you using old syntax "NOT IN", SAP HANA throw error : 

{
"error": "CDS compilation failed In 'NOT IN' at 5-7: Error: ‘IN’ is a reserved word - write ‘![IN]’ instead if you want to use it as name"
}

entry.title: Re: Sap project Cooling period
entry.content: The cooling period for customizations depends upon the priority of each developments. The Go-live critical ones have a shorter cooling period of 1-2 weeks post go-live. One month end closing should be completed in order to measure the success of implementation. For certain transactions where huge data is involved , customers do consider a cooling period of 1-2 months.

entry.title: Re: sap.ui.table.Table disable row selecter for condition rows
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community 👋,

You have a TreeTable with a list of employees.
If the employee's status is "Inactive", that row should:

  • Appear faded

  • Not be selectable

  • Show a tooltip with the reason


    1. Define Table in XML View

<ui:TreeTable
    id="idEmployeeTable"
    rows="{/employees}"
    selectionMode="MultiToggle"
    selectionBehavior="Row"
    rowSelectionChange=".onRowSelect"
    enableSelectAll="false"
    alternateRowColors="true">

    <ui:columns>
        <ui:Column>
            <Label text="Employee Name" />
            <ui:template>
                <Text text="{name}" />
            </ui:template>
        </ui:Column>
        <ui:Column>
            <Label text="Status" />
            <ui:template>
                <Text text="{status}" />
            </ui:template>
        </ui:Column>
    </ui:columns>
</ui:TreeTable>

2. Disable Row Selection for Inactive Employees (after table renders)

onAfterRendering: function () {
    setTimeout(() => {
        var oTable = this.byId("idEmployeeTable");
        if (!oTable) return;

        var aRows = oTable.getRows();
        aRows.forEach((oRow, i) => {
            var oContext = oRow.getBindingContext();
            if (!oContext) return;

            var status = oContext.getProperty("status");
            var name = oContext.getProperty("name");

            if (status === "Inactive") {
                var $row = $(oRow.getDomRef());
                var $checkbox = $(`#${oTable.getId()}-rowsel${i}`);

                // Hide checkbox
                $checkbox.css("visibility", "hidden");

                // Dim the row and block selection
                $row.css({
                    "pointer-events": "none",
                    "opacity": "0.5",
                    "background-color": "#f8d7da"
                });

                // Add tooltip
                $row.attr("title", `Row disabled: ${name} is inactive.`);
            }
        });
    }, 0); // wait until table fully renders
}

Explanation

Part What It Does
setTimeout(..., 0)Waits for the table to fully render
getRows()Gets all currently visible rows
getDomRef()Gets the DOM element for direct styling
pointer-events: nonePrevents click/hover/select
visibility: hidden (on checkbox)Hides the selection checkbox
opacity / background-colorMakes the row look disabled
titleAdds explanation on hover

entry.title: Re: How to split SAP Business One Database
entry.content: This is discontinued in HANA

entry.title: Re: Setting up Principal Propagation
entry.content: Is there a way to configure the same for the Web dispatchers?

entry.title: Re: "No stock posting posting possible for this material"
entry.content: Check whether you have the required authorization to increase the stock.  This you can do by executing /nsu53 tcode after you got the error

entry.title: SAP Analytics cloud planning - Data action issue
entry.content:
Hi, I’m facing an issue with a Data Action where users select a start and end time period to perform employee transfers. However, the Data Action is currently running even on closed periods—meaning the actual periods—where I don’t want the new data to appear. The problem is that I’m scoping the time dimension based on user-selected parameters rather than using version properties, so the Data Action affects both actual and planning periods indiscriminately.

To solve this, I need a way to restrict the Data Action so that it only updates data for planning periods within the user’s selected range and completely excludes any actual (closed) periods, even if they fall between the start and end dates. For example, if the user selects January to June 2025, but January to March 2025 are actual periods, the Data Action should only process April to June 2025, leaving the actual periods untouched.  i have locked data at model level but i dont want to show the user any data movements on actual periods. Can anyone please help.

@N1kh1l 

Regards,

Lakshmi

entry.title: Re: HTML5 APP run failed in BTP Trial Account
entry.content:
i think,

1. if i use a odata from sap, i should not deploy to cloud foundry.maybe this two parts can not connect directly.  HTML5 APP <-> BTP Odata.

2.I should deploy it to the target : ABAP, when i use command: npx fiori add deploy-config.

i am not sure if my point right, still need expert to give the explanation.

thanks!!

entry.title: Re: Material blocking for billing in SAP S/4 Public
entry.content:
It is not clear what is the Business reason that those materials can be delivered but should not be invoiced? Is it like Sample sales or Free Sales ?  Whatever be the reasons, with the standard configuration, you can try.

Have a new Item Category Group which can be assigned to materials that are created with that material type.  Then, assign an item category in vov4 and this item category should be Not Relevant for billing. Setting Billing Block is not a best approach I feel, as there is every chance that certain deliveries would be billed if this billing block is removed erroneously.

entry.title: Re: ABAP2XLSX demo programs

Thanks

Bharath

entry.title: Re: How to split SAP Business One Database
entry.content: Have you tried shrink data volume size in HANA?

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics cloud planning - Data action issue
entry.content:
@pragnya_m 

"For example, if the user selects January to June 2025, but January to March 2025 are actual periods," - How will the system know January to March is Actual period ? Either create some flag to indicate what periods are actual or you can also check if Actual data exists. I am not sure of you model setup but you just need to make the system understand what periods are actual.

IF RESULTLOOKUP([d/version]="public.Actual") !=NULL THEN

ELSE

<Put your logic here>

ENDIF

Hope this helps !!

Nikhil

entry.title: My processes(My requests) App will not show anything in SAP Build Work Zone with BTP Free trial
entry.content:
Hello experts

I am exploring SAP Build Work Zone and process automation with BTP free trial. I set up work zone Business site that can trigger a Process which was defined in Build Process automation and also monitor the progress of approvals performed in both Public and Shared environment with my inbox and Monitor workflow Apps in Work zone.

However, My processes app will not be able to show any Process details but loading eternally. Am I missing some configurations or this is happing simply because Work zone standard option or free trial is not supporting My processes(My requests) App?

I checked required configuration in SAP help and seems everything is set. I appreciate any help or thoughts 🙂

Kind Regards,

Eitaro

P.S.
These two sites helped me set up Work zone and Business sites.
Configure SAP Build Work Zone, Standard Edition | SAP Tutorials

Run a Business Process From the Business Site | SAP Tutorials

entry.title: Re: How to read a context variable in formcalc
entry.content: It worked for me with ".value": if ( $record.FIEDL.value == "X") { this.rawValue = "abc"; } (JavaScript)

entry.title: Fiori Launchpad - Transaction code in tiles not working for non S4 core systems?
entry.content:
Hi,

we have multiple S4 systems on 2023 FPS3 with embedded launchpads. For some systems the transaction code in tile functionality does not seem to work, 

Replicate Technical Catalogs per Business Catalog | SAP Help Portal

On our standard S4 ERP System the parameter REPLICATION_TCODE_TO_TILE_INFO and the report did work as expected. We have now transaction codes in the tiles for classic transactions.

But in the BW4 or the MDG system no transaction codes are shown even if the systems have new my home activated and are up to date. The report also does not show errors.

Did someone have a similar problems? Is it a technical catalog type problem?

Best regards Richard

entry.title: Add validation to the "Switch between Display and Change" button in BP T-code.
entry.content:
Hello SAP Experts,

I have implemented the BADI BUPA_LOCK to display an error message if an attachment does not exist for a Business Partner (BP) customer in the S/4HANA system. I would like your input on whether this is the correct and recommended approach, or if there is a better way to fulfill this requirement.

Requirement:

In S/4HANA, during the use of Transaction Code BP, if a Business Partner (BP) customer does not have any attachments, the system should display an error message to the user.

When the user clicks on "Switch between Display and Change" button in BP T-code, the system should check for attachment. If none are found, an error message should be displayed. Screenshots are attached below. If attachment exists, then system will allow to make changes in BP.

In ECC, similar requirement was handled in program SAPMF02D (Transaction Code XD02). SAPMF02D is obsoleted in S/4 HANA (SAP Note Ref -2606686) . 

Implementation Details:

  • BADI Used: BUPA_LOCK
  • Interface: IF_EX_BUPA_LOCK
  • Implementing Class: ZCL_IM_MDS_BUPA_LOCK
  • Method Implemented: IF_EX_BUPA_LOCK~LOCK

Test Case:

  • Business Partner: 295
  • Customer Number: 44
  • Scenario: No attachments currently exist.

Currently it is successfully fulfilling the business requirement.

My Question:

While the current implementation works as expected, I wanted to make sure if using BUPA_LOCK is the right place to perform this kind of validation.

  • Is this BADI intended for such validations?
  • Could there be a more suitable enhancement point or BADI for checking attachments in the BP transaction?
  • Are there any potential issues or risks with using BUPA_LOCK for this purpose?

Any guidance, recommendations, or best practices you can share would be greatly appreciated. Thank you in advance!

Attached the screenshots for reference.

entry.title: Request to Include Check Number and Payee Name in Bank Reconciliation Report.
entry.content:
Dear All,

I would like to request a modification to the Bank Reconciliation Report in SAP Business One.

Specifically, I would like the following changes :

  1. Include the Check Number in the report for better tracking of payments so we can easily track our payments on window.

  2. Replace the Payee Code with the Payee Name for easier identification of payee.

Please note that we have already utilized the Ref1, Ref2, and Ref3 fields in our current setup, so using these fields for this purpose is not a feasible solution.

Kindly suggest if anyone have any solution related to this.

Regards,

Akshay

entry.title: Re: SAP Business One Mobile Service Traffic Abnormality

Login to the Integration Framework, Disable Detailed Log as it generates large log files for each transaction.

entry.title: Re: MRP Run for FG Material With strategy 40 and strategy...
entry.content:
For the strategy group, you should only maintain for the Finihsed material, you should not maintain strategy group  for the Semi-finished/ Raw  material, just keep the strategy group = Blank for the SF and RW materials.

For exampl: FG Stategy group = 50.

Create the PIR for FG.

For the Semi-finished/ Raw  material, just keep the strategy group = Blank,

if the Ind/Coll requirement =1, Run MRP, it will not create the planned order/ PR until the SO was created for FG.

if the  Ind/Coll requirement =2, Run MRP，it will create the planed order.

entry.content:

When SO down payment with installment, how to make this scenario in SO?

Billing plan with down payment seems not allow to add additional installment value as system check if total amount is the same with down payment value when choose billing rule 4 or 5.

entry.title: Re: Subcontracting value should be added in material pric...
entry.content:
Thanks @mafu  @Chuma 

Let me show you scenario and what exactly customer wants

Material : HFS3238009530038

Price : 81000

Warehouse Stock

 

 

4 TO qty. is available at MRM1 Sloc, Batch 366

 

 

Creating PO

 

 BOM

 

 

Condition

 

 

Transfer Posting

 

Stock overview after transfer posting

 

 

GRN of Subcontracted Material

 

 

Accounting Document

 

 

Final Stock

 

 

Value of stock

 

 

Requirement: When I receive subcontracted material from supplier, price of that particular material (Batch 372) should be 81200 (81000 + 200). Here MFI1 has 5 qty., but value should be 405200, instead of 405000.

Also, I want app name where value against batch number should be visible. In above app (Display Warehouse Stock) I can only see cumulative qty. in storage location. And in app “Display Stock Overview” I can’t see value.

Note: Subcontracted material code is same as BOM component.

Is it possible in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud? If No, then please help me with work around.

entry.title: Re: 🌟 Welcome to the SAP Community Group – Middle East! 🌟
entry.content:
Hello,

I’m Yasir Ahmed Shaikh, with 22+ years of experience in ERP delivery and project management. I specialize in SAP, Oracle, and IT transformation programs. Over the years, I’ve led large-scale implementations across multiple industries, including banking, FMCG, and manufacturing.

I am based in Dubai and working with a SAP Partner for digital transformation journeys.

Happy to be part of this group and looking forward to learning, sharing, and connecting with you all!

entry.title: Re: Retention policy of messages
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, the Message Monitoring Overview app (part of the Integration & Exception Monitoring for cloud) follows a standard retention policy :

  • Individual (single) messages are retained for 14 days from creation. After this, they're deleted permanently you’ll need to act within that window to view or reprocess them.

 

  • If messages are correlated as part of larger message chains, you can enable the option "Retain messages until all correlated messages are deleted". This ensures correlated messages stay visible until the entire set expires.

 

  • Once individual messages expire, their details are removed; however, the system keeps aggregated message data. This summary information can be retained for much longer up to 120 months (10 years), depending on system memory and configuration.

 

To summarize :

Message Type

Retention Period

Notes

Single messages

14 days

Not extendable

Correlated messages*

Up to 14 days

Until all linked expire

Aggregated message data

Configurable up to 120 months

Based on memory settings

* Correlated messages last until the last linked message expires.

Attention : this 14‑day limit for single messages is fixed and cannot be extended by end users

 

I hope this will help you answer your question.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: How to Configure SFTP Authentication in SAP Cloud Integration (CPI) – A Step-by-Step Guide
entry.content:
SFTP Configuration and Usage Guide

 

* Access the SFTP Platform

  • Visit the URL:https://sftpcloud.io/tools/free-sftp-server
  • Click on the Login button located at the top right corner.

* Sign Up / Log In

  • Use the 'Sign Up / Log In' option to register or enter your account.

* Create SFTP instance

  • After a successful login, To Create New SFTP instance.

  • Next, Open the SFTP instance, click on 'Users', and then go to 'Connection Details’.

After configure SFTP server, we will have some info of it as

  • User name
  • Password phrase
  • Host name
  • SFTP Port

* Install WinSCP

  • Visit the URL:https://winscp.net/eng/download.php
  • Open WinSCP and login using the Host Name, User Name, and Password from the SFTP server.

  • Here, we create a directory or file in WinSCP to upload or download files from the SFTP server.

* Create Known Host in CPI
  1. Go to integration Suite Application
  2. Go to Design integration flow
  3. Go to Monitor item in left menu
  4. Go to Connectivity Test

  • Open Notepad, paste the Host Key, and save the file with an appropriate name (e.g., hostkey.txt).
  • Add this known host into CPI

* Create User Credentials

  1. Go to integration Suite Application
  2. Go to Design integration flow
  3. Go to Monitor item in left menu
  4. Go to Security Material
  5. Click on create

* To check the user credentials, go to 'Connectivity Test' and click on 'SSH'.

1. Username/Password :-

 

  • SFTP SENDER IFLOW

     Create/Configure an Integration Flow

  1. We are using HTTPS to send the data {\"productIdentifier\":\"HT-2000\"} because this value needs to be passed to the OData service.

  1. The JSON to XML Converter is used to convert incoming JSON data into XML, as required by the OData service.

  1. Use the Content Modifier to assign information to headers.

  1. Use the Request-Reply step to fetch product details from the OData service.

  1. The OData service is being accessed from the SAP Business Accelerator Hub (api.sap.com).

In the SAP Business Accelerator Hub, navigate to APIs → ODATA V2, then search for 'Product Master'.

 Click on "Show API Key" to get the key needed for authentication.

In the API Specification, you can download the OData EDMX, which is useful when integrating in SAP Cloud Integration (CPI) for OData service modeling.

In the Configuration Details section, the Sandbox URL helps in forming the complete endpoint URL by replacing the {host} placeholder with the actual hostname when configuring the OData connection in SAP Cloud Integration (CPI).

  1. Now in the connection, use the Sandbox URL as the Address.

We are using the data {\"productIdentifier\":\"HT-2000\"} to retrieve the specific product details from the OData service.

  1. Next, use the XML to CSV Converter to send data to the receiver, as the SFTP server only accepts CSV format.
  2. The SFTP Adapter is configured at the receiver end to deliver the converted CSV file.The directory and address are taken from the SFTP server, the file name is created dynamically, and the credentials are retrieved from the security material.
  3. Next, save and deploy the integration. Then, open Manage Integration Content and copy the Endpoint URL.

     

  4. Go to Postman, select the GET method, and paste the endpoint URL.

In the Auth type, select OAuth 2.0, and provide the Client ID, Client Secret, and Token URL from the service instance. Then, click Get New Access Token.

Provide the body data, click on Send, and view the result.

  1.  Now, go to WinSCP, where you can see the CSV file or data in the specified directory.

 

 

  • SFTP RECEIVER IFLOW

Create/Configure an Integration Flow

  1. Open or create an Integration Flow (iFlow).
  2. Connect the sender participant to Start and select the SFTP as adapter. Configure the SFTP adapter as per requirements.

Make sure that the SFTP credentials are deployed in the below path

Overview --> Manage Security --> Security Material --> Create/Upload Credentials

Check the connectivity test of SFTP adapter in Test Connectivity in the below path

Overview --> Manage Security --> Connectivity Tests --> SSH

Copy the Host Key and paste it in notepad & deploy it in Known Hosts(SSH) file in the below path.

Overview --> Manage Security --> Security Material --> Upload Known Hosts(SSH)

  1.  In the processing tab also configure as per your requirement whether you want to Archive, Keep File and Process Again.

  1. In the scheduler tab also we need to choose how many times this particular interface needs to be run.

  1. Use the CSV to XML Converter to transform the incoming CSV file into structured XML format.

  1. After saving and deploying the integration, go to Monitor → Message Processing → Message Processing Run, click on the end of the run to view the XML data.

2. PUBLIC KEY :-

 

  • Create SSH KEY :
  1. Go to Monitor > Integrations and APIs, open Manage Keystore, click Create, and select SSH Key to generate a new SSH key.

  1. After creating the SSH key, click on the three dots (︙) and select Download Public OpenSSH Key.

Now open the SFTP server. In Windows PowerShell, run the following commands:

  • cd .\Downloads\ – to change the directory to Downloads
  • ls – to list the files and confirm your SSH key is there
  • cat <your_ssh_key_name>.pub – to display the public SSH key

  1. Copy the displayed public SSH key, open the SFTP server, navigate to Public Keys, paste the key, and click Save.

  • SFTP SENDER IFLOW

Using the same flows, just changing the authentication method from username/password to private key.

  1. Open the receiver SFTP adapter, select 'Public Key' for authentication, and provide the private key alias and the user name from the SFTP server.

  1. Next, save and deploy the integration. Then, open Manage Integration Content and copy the Endpoint URL.

  1. In the Auth type, select OAuth 2.0, and provide the Client ID, Client Secret, and Token URL from the service instance. Then, click Get New Access Token. Provide the body data, click on Send, and view the result.

  1. Now, go to WinSCP, where you can see the CSV file or data in the specified directory.

 

  • SFTP RECEIVER IFLOW
  1. Open the sender SFTP adapter, select 'Public Key' for authentication, and provide the private key alias and the user name from the SFTP server.

  1. After saving and deploying the integration, go to Monitor → Message Processing → Message Processing Run, click on the end of the run to view the XML data.

3. DUAL :-

 

  • SFTP SENDER IFOW

     Using the same flows, just changing the authentication method to Dual.

  1. Open the receiver SFTP adapter, select 'Dual' for authentication, and provide the private key alias and the Credential name.

  1. Next, save and deploy the integration. Then, open Manage Integration Content and copy the Endpoint URL.

  1. In the Auth type, select OAuth 2.0, and provide the Client ID, Client Secret, and Token URL from the service instance. Then, click Get New Access Token. Provide the body data, click on Send, and view the result.

  1. Now, go to WinSCP, where you can see the CSV file or data in the specified directory.

 

  • SFTP RECEIVER IFOW

      Using the same flows, just changing the authentication method to Dual.

  1. Open the receiver SFTP adapter, select 'Dual' for authentication, and provide the private key alias and the Credential name.

  1. After saving and deploying the integration, go to Monitor → Message Processing → Message Processing Run, click on the end of the run to view the XML data.

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics cloud planning - Data action issue

entry.title: Re: Announcing SAP Inside Track Kolkata – 2nd August 2025

entry.title: Re: Sum up values of MDF object on parent object
entry.content:
Hi @Sri_bs ,

I don't know whether this would fit your use case but you could have the amount field on the grandparent as a transient field and use the sum-of-collection function in an onLoad rule for this transient field.

Kind regards, Heike

entry.title: ABSENCE CARRY OVER
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I want to carry forward the leftover quota balance of quota type 15 to 16, for which I wrote attached two PCR's (ZQEC - To Carry forward the balance on 31st Dec & ZQEU - To update the leftover balance on 1st Jan) and placed both the PCR's as shown in the attachment (Schema).

In the update PCR (ZQEU), I have used the syntax function as HRS=FR15C, which should actually carry forward the leftover balance, however, in my case it is carry forwarding the quota balance that was used (Quota Deduction value) within the current year (TimeType ZQEC = -16, the deduction value, instead of 104)

Kindly please have a look at the configuration and suggest me where it's going wrong.

Many thanks in advance.

Sai

entry.title: IDOC from EWM_TU to carrier
entry.content:
Hello EWM Experts,

I have a requirement to send an IDOC to the carrier system from EWM, from TU at post GI

I see same question some years ago, but no answer

Do you know if is possible? Standard way 

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Change GL and Movement Type manually when request mat...
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, the ability to manually change the G/L account and movement type for reservations in a Plant Maintenance (PM) order is limited by standard system behavior and configuration constraints. Here's what you need to know :

 

What you can configure or influence

1. Movement Type Determination

The movement type (e.g., 261) is automatically determined based on:

  • Order type (e.g., YBA1 – Corrective Maintenance)
  • Item category
  • Material type
  • Stock type (e.g., unrestricted)

To change this :

  • You would need to adjust the configuration in the SSCUI app "Manage Your Solution" if you are using a custom movement type or in the CBC tenant (Central Business Configuration)
  • However, in Public Cloud, the movement type for standard PM orders (261 for goods issue) is usually not configurable per order type.

There is no direct UI field to change movement type manually in the reservation line within the maintenance order app.

 

2. G/L Account Determination

The G/L account is determined via Account Assignment (OBYC logic) and valuation class of the material master.

To change the G/L account :

  • You need to either:
    • Use a different material with a different valuation class, which will hit a different G/L account.
    • Or (only if allowed in your system) manually override the account via Account Assignment if you implement a custom BAdI (not always possible in public cloud).

 

Alternative option via BAdI

If you need to customize the G/L account or movement type logic dynamically, you can consider:

BAdI: MFG_ORDER_POST_EE

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud, some extensibility is possible via in-app extensibility (BAdIs).

Check for BAdIs like:

  • Order Operation Material Component (for components in PM orders)
  • BADI_RESERVATION (if exposed)
  • BADI_MM_ACCT_ASSIGNMENT – for accounting determination (depends on release version and scope item)

Use the "Custom Logic" app and search for available enhancement spots related to:

  • Maintenance Order
  • Reservation
  • Material Movement

 

Limitations in Public Cloud

  • You cannot customize OBYC config directly.
  • You cannot create or modify movement types freely like in On-Premise.
  • Manual override of movement type in PM reservation line item is not available through UI.

 

I hope this will help you answer your questions.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Process Automation - Transition guidelines from Public Environment to Shared Environme
entry.content:
Hello Alex,

Thank you for the great and informative blog post!

We have a quick question regarding the recent changes.
How will these updates impact Business Application Studio Workflows?

Currently, in Business Application Studio, we don’t have the option to select the target environment for deployment, it always deploys directly to the Public Environment. With the new changes, what should we expect going forward?

Also, is there now a way to deploy to a shared environment from within Business Application Studio?

Thank you very much in advance for your clarifications!

entry.title: Re: How to read Table AB in Payroll run to idetify specia...
entry.content:
You can include a special rule for your absence type in table V_T554C. In this way, when an absence is getting processed through function PAB, you can define special processing for it.

It worked for me.

Hope this helps!!

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics cloud planning - Data action issue
entry.content:
What you still did not mention how will the system know what is a closed period. Do you have any flags for close period. You need to define the criteria for closed period. 

Even if you are running the DA on Forecast version you can still build filtering logic based on Actual version. As you are not specifying what is a closed period, I asked you to check if there is data in Actual version ( logic that period is Actual period)

entry.title: Re: ABAP CDS Cheat Sheet: Amounts and Quantities in ABAP CDS
entry.content:
Hi @albertosimeoni 

In view entities, reference fields must be defined locally to ensure the correct decimal shift of currency fields. External reference fields are not possible.

In classic CDS views, there were no checks for this.

Your observation is correct and we cannot offer any workaround. Issues has been addressed with dev team.

Sorry for any inconveniences caused.

Best 

Andrea

entry.title: Code Jam schedule plan for With SAP Build, Create Apps, Processes, and Business Sites Without Coding
entry.content:
Dear Code Jam Team,

We are a SAP Partner interested in organizing a code jam focused on SAP Build, Create Apps, Processes, and Business Sites Without Coding.

We experienced that the SAP Code Jam events provide a great opportunity for us developers and our customers developers to learn and develop our skills in multiple technologies, while also connecting with each other and sharing ideas.

As our previous code jams with SAP were a success, we would appreciate your assistance in organizing this event, as well as connecting us with an SAP expert who can lead the Code Jam session. We now would like to organize the code jam for 'with SAP Build, Create Apps, Processes and Business Sites without Coding'

We propose hosting the event in September 2025 at our two offices 1 in Bengaluru  EC 360 degree office and 1 in Pune Balewadi Highstreet office, with an estimated number of participants of 25.

Please confirm on dates options possible to you.

Thank you for your time and consideration. We look forward to the opportunity to work with you in organizing again a successful Code Jam event.

Sincerely,
Ashutosh

entry.title: Quantity field is not updating in ACDOCA table during KO88/CO88 settlement
entry.content:
Hello All,

I have been trying to identify the reason for not updating the quantity filed in document ledger view from KO88/CO88 settlement process. Quanity field is updating in ACDOCA table or in FAGLFLEXA table during all the other production process such as goods issue and confirmation. But during the settlement process not quanity field is updating in the system. I have tested this scenario both in S4hana and ECC system and got the similar results in both the test case. Only BSEG table us updating the quantity field during the settlement run. Does any one has any idea about this issue?

entry.title: Add validation to the "Switch between Display and Change" button in BP T-code.
entry.content:
Hello SAP Experts,

I have implemented the BADI BUPA_LOCK to display an error message if an attachment does not exist for a Business Partner (BP) customer in the S/4HANA system. I would like your input on whether this is the correct and recommended approach, or if there is a better way to fulfill this requirement.

Requirement:

In S/4HANA, during the use of Transaction Code BP, if a Business Partner (BP) customer does not have any attachments, the system should display an error message to the user.

When the user clicks on "Switch between Display and Change" button in BP T-code, the system should check for attachment. If none are found, an error message should be displayed. Screenshots are attached below. If attachment exists, then system will allow to make changes in BP.

In ECC, similar requirement was handled in program SAPMF02D (Transaction Code XD02). SAPMF02D is obsoleted in S/4 HANA (SAP Note Ref -2606686) . 

Implementation Details:

  • BADI Used: BUPA_LOCK
  • Interface: IF_EX_BUPA_LOCK
  • Implementing Class: ZCL_IM_MDS_BUPA_LOCK
  • Method Implemented: IF_EX_BUPA_LOCK~LOCK

Test Case:

  • Business Partner: 295
  • Customer Number: 44
  • Scenario: No attachments currently exist.

Currently it is successfully fulfilling the business requirement.

My Question:

While the current implementation works as expected, I wanted to make sure if using BUPA_LOCK is the right place to perform this kind of validation.

  • Is this BADI intended for such validations?
  • Could there be a more suitable enhancement point or BADI for checking attachments in the BP transaction?
  • Are there any potential issues or risks with using BUPA_LOCK for this purpose?

Any guidance, recommendations, or best practices you can share would be greatly appreciated. Thank you in advance!

Attached the screenshots for reference.

entry.title: OTP based login in SAC dashboard
entry.content:

I am trying to create an OTP based login in my SAC dashboard where i will be able to see my SAC dashboards based on provided OTP and after clicking submit button i will be navigated to my dashboards with proper data filtered based on the OTP used.  Does anyone have any idea on how to achieve this.

entry.title: Re: ADS: com.adobe.ProcessingException: com.adobe.Processin(200101)
entry.content: In my case, the ADS java instance was not able to answer back the RFC from SAP ABAP due to wrong client info in ADS Java config at FP_ICF_DATA_<SID>, correction in it gets the issue resolved.

entry.content: Hi Shane, thank you for this video! BR Heli

entry.title: Re: ADS: com.adobe.ProcessingException: com.adobe.Process...
entry.content: In my case, the ADS java instance was not able to answer back the RFC from SAP ABAP due to wrong client info in ADS Java config at FP_ICF_DATA_, correction in it gets the issue resolved.

entry.title: Re: SAP Analytics Cloud - Inverse Formula planning year c...
entry.content:
Hi @guenay ,

I am able to plan on Year level aside from Period/Year level with inverse formula. I could be wrong, however, I don't think your issue is related to inverse formula. Given that I didn't put any special treatment for inverse formula in my side.

However, reason for unplannable data can be varied, hence why you can see the reason for yourself. You can just enable it in the table (just like the below image).

Hopefully it helps.

Regards,

Ricky

entry.title: Fiori Elements Action: Navigation Not Triggering After Returning New Instance
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I'm working with SAP Fiori Elements (V4) and CAP, and I have an action defined using @ui.DataFieldForAction that creates a new record with updated effective dates. The backend correctly returns a new instance (confirmed via HTTP 200 OK with full entity data), and I've set navigateToInstance: true in the manifest.json.

Despite this, the navigation to the object page of the newly created instance is not happening.

Here’s what I’ve already checked:

  • The action returns a single entity with a new ID.
  • Object page target is defined for target entity.
  • navigateToInstance: true is set under actions.
  • Backend response includes all key fields and metadata context.
// <service.js> - when action triggers, this below is executed
        const { ID } = req.params[0];
        const { targetentity } = cds.entities('MyService')
        if (ID) {
            const record = await SELECT.one.from(targetentity).where({ ID });
            const newID = uuid();
            console.log("New ID", newID);

            if (record) {
                // Logic to change the effective date
                record.effective_start_date = req.data.eStartDate;
                record.effective_end_date = req.data.eEndDate;
                record.ID = newID;
                await INSERT.into(targetentity).entries(record);

                // ✅ Return the new instance explicitly
                const newRecord = await SELECT.one.from(IARuleForDerivations).where({ ID: newID });
                req.reply(newRecord);
/* I try these things also
*  return newRecord
*/ 
            } else {
                throw new Error("Record not found.");
            }
        } else {
            throw new Error("ID is undefined.");
        }
// <service.cds> Entity Projection with actions
@odata.draft.enabled
entity targetentity as projection on db.targetentity
   actions {
    action changeEffectiveDateToNewRecord(
@UI.ParameterDefaultValue: in.effective_start_date
eStartDate : db.targetentity: effective_start_date,

@UI.ParameterDefaultValue: in.effective_end_date
eEndDate : db.targetentity: effective_end_date) 
returns targetentity;
                };

  Is there anything else I need to configure in the manifest.json or annotations to ensure navigation is triggered correctly?

Thanks in advance,
Kirubakaran K

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA side-by-side extension with ABAP?
entry.content:
If you are interested into a deep dive hands-on for an ABAP based side-by-side implementation have a look at our Basic Trial offering:

SAP Build and ABAP Cloud Basic Trial Offering - SAP Community

entry.title: Whitescreen after logging in Public cloud with Zebra scanner (via Velocity)
entry.content:
Hi all,

I’m facing an issue when trying to connect to SAP Public Cloud using Velocity on a Zebra MC330K handheld scanner (Android 8).

After launching the Velocity session and entering login credentials, the screen turns completely white and doesn’t proceed. This issue cannot be debugged directly in Velocity.

Here’s what I’ve already checked:

  • The root cause seems to be an outdated Chromium WebView (version 67).

  • I tested with the standard Chromium browser deployed via Zebra StageNow/ADB (also version 67), and the issue persists.

  • The device is running with Zebra Enterprise Home Screen, so I have no direct access to standard Android settings or the Play Store to update Chromium.

Has anyone faced something similar?
Is there a recommended approach to update the Chromium WebView on these Zebra devices — or an alternative way to run SAP Fiori apps in such constrained environments? Could the solution be try to push Zebra enterprise browser to the scanner?

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA side-by-side extension with ABAP?
entry.content:
Most API's are nowadays based on OData rather than using RFC. 
So I have added a link to our Basic Trial Offering that nicely shows a side by side extension using OData based services.

entry.title: Re: ABAP2XLSX demo programs
entry.content:
Thank you for the screenshot, it makes it clearer for me what the problem is. The packages have "abapGit" in their description, so it looks like you have imported abapGit as ZABAP2XLSX here. To fix this you need to clear this package by uninstalling abapGit from this package first, you can move the ZABAPGIT_STANDALONE to another package (e.g. $ABAPGIT) before. You don't need to install the developer version again, the standalone version should be sufficient.

Then you can install ABAP2XLSX into the ZABAP2XLSX package and finally you can install/activate the demo package (maybe use another package name like $ABAP2XLSXDEMO as recommended in the ABAP2XLSX guideline to make a clearer difference to the normal, non-local ABAP2XLSX package).

entry.title: Re: Data Intelligence - Considerations for Cloud or On-premises/BYOL installation
entry.content:
if we want to start using SAP Data Intelligence now, is it a good option considering the end of maintenance in 2026?  Are there any successor already available?

End of Mainstream Maintenance:31.12.2026

entry.title: Re: How to directly select the data of SAP BW CUBE ?
entry.content:
Hi Charlie

 

Use LISTCUBE or the default query in RSRT.

Otherwise you would need to join the Fact table with the corresponding SID tables of the included characteristics.

 

br

Roland

entry.title: Re: How to directly select the data of SAP BW CUBE ?

entry.title: Re: Free Goods Determination
entry.content:
Hello,
I raised this to SAP as it was urgent and found the solution.

As per this we have to change in the field catalog, Document structure in Access sequence from KOMK(header) to KOMP(item).
Before


Here go to Field catalog


Select this one
After

and its Done.
Regards,
Praddep More.

However, in the jungle of BTP I lack a best practise how to properly secure such a microservice with the Features of BTP.

 

Best Regards

Tobias

entry.title: Re: Quantity field is not updating in ACDOCA table during...
entry.content:
Hello,
Settlement (KO88/CO88) is primarily a value flow process. Its main purpose is to distribute or transfer costs (and sometimes revenues) from a cost object (like a production order or internal order) to various other controlling objects (like CO-PA, G/L accounts, fixed assets, etc.). It's about "where the money goes," not typically about "how much product was moved."     mybkexperience com
Goods movements (Goods Issue, Goods Receipt, Confirmations) are inherently quantity-based. When you issue raw materials or receive finished goods, you are physically moving a quantity of material, which directly impacts inventory and cost of goods manufactured/sold.

Best Regards

entry.title: SAP UI5 components in SAP Build apps
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am working and playing around in SAP Build apps(Mobile and Web application). I wondered that is there any way to use the SAP UI5 components in  SAP Build apps?.

Looking forward to hear some interesting things about it.

Thank you.
